## Track

<!-- Pick one. See CONTRIBUTING.md for the two-track rule. -->

- [ ] **Correction** — fixes a wrong claim/number/sample/link (evidence below)
- [ ] **Proposal implementation** — wording for a discussed proposal: closes #___

## What claim changes

<!-- One or two sentences: what does the document say after this PR that it
     didn't before (or vice versa)? "Wording only, no claim change" is a
     valid answer for typo/clarity fixes. -->

## Evidence

<!-- Corrections: repo link / command output / benchmark.
     Proposal PRs: link the discussion where the change was agreed. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] No claim merged with an unverified number (`[verify]` markers resolved)
- [ ] Style rules hold (CONTRIBUTING.md §Style) — no tone "improvements",
      digressions and hedges preserved
- [ ] Cross-references use boundary names and relative links; map/uncertainty
      links updated if claims moved
- [ ] Page status lines still accurate (promotion/demotion is a proposal,
      not a side effect)
- [ ] Commit message says what claim changed, not "update docs"
